page: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8488C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz and includes a 105 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2015.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 33.37 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show relative weaknesses. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is in the bottom 25% at 3775.26, and the Passmark memory score of 1733.21 indicates lower memory efficiency under load. The CPU features advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which support workloads like low-latency vector math and cryptography, as reflected in average scores for Passmark encryption and ray tracing.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.xlarge offers a modern DDR5 memory architecture and dedicated compute resources at a standard general-purpose tier, making it a stable option for workloads that do not require high-throughput local storage. Due to its strong single-core execution but weaker multi-core scaling and database performance, this server is best suited for application servers, static web serving, text processing, and lightweight single-threaded tasks rather than heavy database operations or parallelized software compilation.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2015.0 and high single-threaded gzip compression throughput. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth under heavy read operations show weaker performance profiles. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPUs, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard workloads requiring modern CPU architectures without extreme parallelization. It is suited for static web serving, text processing, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Nitro hypervisor virtualization, delivering strong single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.xlarge is an Intel-powered general-purpose server with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A general-purpose virtual server featuring Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and strong single-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM
Strong single-core performance in text processing and gzip compression
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 advanced instruction sets
Weaker multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth under heavy loads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for static web serving, text processing, and general application hosting
